intent: a personal dashboard for tracking long-term health experiments

body.intent: a personal dashboard for tracking long-term health experiments

draft.title: a personal dashboard for tracking long-term health experiments
draft.summary: Drafting your prototype from the swipes…
draft.html:
a personal dashboard for tracking long-term health experiments

Building your first draft…

data.intent: a personal dashboard for tracking long-term health experiments

facade.hypothesis: For a health dashboard, users prefer simple icon systems over photographic or photorealistic representations
facade.label: Minimal line drawings
facade.acceptImplies: Lean into icon-based, schematic visual language for data viz and UI elements
facade.rejectImplies: Explore photograph-based, photorealistic, or rich textured visual approaches

facade.hypothesis: Users prefer soft, ambient visualization of health metrics over stark clinical data displays
facade.label: Warm glow
facade.acceptImplies: Dashboard should use warm colors, rounded elements, and gentle typography to make health tracking feel encouraging rather than medical
facade.rejectImplies: Users want clear, neutral, or cool-toned interfaces that feel objective and scientific rather than emotionally designed

evidence.hypothesis: For a health dashboard, users prefer simple icon systems over photographic or photorealistic representations
evidence.implication: Lean into icon-based, schematic visual language for data viz and UI elements

facade.hypothesis: Users prefer fluid, eased animations between dashboard screens over instant jumps
facade.label: Smooth slide transitions
facade.acceptImplies: Explore choreographed micro-interactions, momentum-based scrolling, and cross-page transitions that feel continuous
facade.rejectImplies: Explore instant-load architecture, minimal animation overhead, snappy instant state changes with visual stability instead

facade.hypothesis: A tone that celebrates small wins and gently prompts reflection without feeling clinical or preachy will resonate more than neutral, data-only interfaces for health tracking.
facade.label: Encouraging nudges
facade.acceptImplies: The dashboard voice should be warm, congratulatory, and motivational—using positive language like 'Great week!' or 'Ready to log today?'
facade.rejectImplies: The dashboard should maintain clinical distance, neutral language, and minimal personality—focusing on data presentation over tone.

facade.hypothesis: The user prefers organic, flowing visual feedback over rigid numerical displays—suggesting a preference for warm, human-centered atmosphere over cold data presentation
facade.label: Soft progress rings
facade.acceptImplies: Lean into rounded, animated progress indicators; warm color palettes; hand-drawn or soft iconography; data presented as gentle curves rather than sharp charts
facade.rejectImplies: User may prefer clean, minimal data viz; sharp lines and geometric shapes; cool color schemes; precision-focused metrics display; digital-first aesthetic

facade.hypothesis: Users tracking health data over weeks/months prefer dark interfaces to reduce eye strain during frequent dashboard check-ins
facade.label: Dark mode
facade.acceptImplies: Explore dark-first design, high-contrast readouts for experiment metrics, dark backgrounds with bright data highlights
facade.rejectImplies: Explore light or adaptive themes; test whether brightness preference correlates with data density or interaction frequency instead

axes.label: Whether user prefers minimal clinical aesthetics vs. warm, human-centered visual design for health data
Whether user wants data-dense dashboard layout vs. progressive disclosure with one metric at a time
axes.poleA: All at once or step by step?

axes.label: Whether user responds to motivational/encouraging tone vs. neutral, factual reporting voice
Whether user wants celebratory, gamified feedback vs. calm, meditative tracking environment
Whether user prefers raw numbers and charts vs. narrative summaries and insights
Whether user wants instant feedback on actions vs. slow, contemplative animations and transitions

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers minimal clinical aesthetics vs. warm, human-centered visual design for health data
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Iris

scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Prism

scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Lumen

scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Aura

scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Echo

facade.hypothesis: For a health dashboard tracking personal experiments, users prefer dark interfaces that reduce eye strain during frequent checking and feel more modern/premium than light backgrounds
facade.label: Dark background, light text
facade.acceptImplies: Prioritize dark mode as default; use high-contrast light typography; consider dark backgrounds for data visualization to make metrics pop
facade.rejectImplies: Explore light/bright backgrounds as primary; test whether clinical/clean aesthetic (light) resonates more than modern/premium (dark) for health contexts

draft.title: Experiment Tracker Dashboard
draft.summary: A personal health dashboard for monitoring long-term experiments with visual progress tracking, experiment cards, and key metrics at a glance.
